author: maxnick
title: JS API E2E Electron cleanup fails on Windows - Permission denied removing locked electron.exe/dll
signature: rm cannot remove electron.exe Permission denied|Flaky Test

failed_jobs.name: JS API (26)
failed_jobs.symptom: E2E after-all hook fails: rm -rf demo-electron-app-project Permission denied on electron.exe/DLLs

key_errors:
exec error: Error: Command failed: rm -rf demo-electron-app-project
rm: cannot remove 'demo-electron-app-project/node_modules/electron/dist/d3dcompiler_47.dll': Permission denied
rm: cannot remove 'demo-electron-app-project/node_modules/electron/dist/electron.exe': Permission denied
rm: cannot remove 'demo-electron-app-project/node_modules/electron/dist/libEGL.dll': Permission denied
rm: cannot remove 'demo-electron-app-project/node_modules/electron/dist/libGLESv2.dll': Permission denied

1 failing
  1) E2E testing for OpenVINO as an Electron dependency.
       "after all" hook for "should run electron package and verify output":
     Error: Command failed: rm -rf demo-electron-app-project
root_cause: On Windows, the Electron process spawned during the E2E test holds file locks on electron.exe and its dependent DLLs (d3dcompiler_47.dll, libEGL.dll, libGLESv2.dll). The test's 'after all' cleanup hook runs rm -rf immediately after verifying the Electron app output, before Windows fully releases those file handles. This is a race condition in cleanup timing specific to Windows file-locking semantics. The actual E2E functionality tests all pass; only the cleanup fails.
reproduction_steps: Run `npm run test:e2e` in `src/bindings/js/node/` on a Windows runner and observe the 'after all' hook failure when Electron process file locks haven't been fully released.
recommended_actions: Add a delay or retry loop in the 'after all' cleanup hook before running rm -rf on Windows
Use `rimraf` or a Node.js fs.rm with retry options instead of shell rm -rf for cross-platform cleanup
Consider using `taskkill /F /IM electron.exe` before cleanup on Windows to ensure process termination
File a bug/tracking issue for this known recurring flaky test pattern (seen 11 times)
prevention_strategies: Add explicit process termination and file-handle release before directory cleanup in E2E tests on Windows. Use Node.js built-in fs APIs with retry logic for cleanup rather than shell commands.
historical_context: This failure pattern (85df26485ff568d8) has been recorded 10 times previously since 2026-07-09. It is the most frequently recurring flaky test in the Windows CI pipeline.
